---
title: "Schedule"
editor: visual
---

Below is the planned schedule. Dates, topics, and assignments may adjust as needed.

**Class Format:** Class meets Mondays 3:30–4:45 PM in 2439 TNRB. Each class is run as a practice: short whistle segments (film review, chalk talk) followed by live drill time building with Claude Code. Most of your work happens between practices; class time is where we install skills and review film.

**Note:** Fall classes begin Wed, Sep 2, but Labor Day (Sep 7) means our first in-person practice is Mon, Sep 14. Week of Sep 2 is an online setup week. We DO meet Mon, Nov 23 (Thanksgiving break begins Wed, Nov 25). Last day of class is Thu, Dec 10.

| Practice | Date | Part | Emphasis (Chalk Talk) | Drill (Live Build) | Assessments |
|----|------|------|----------------------|--------------------|-------------|
| 0 | Week of Sep 2 (online) | Setup | Course overview, what is PM in the AI era | Claude Code setup, terminal basics, first deploy | Setup checklist |
| 1 | Mon, Sep 14 | Foundations | The practice model, PM history, the AIRE loop, AI disruption | Git fundamentals, company repo from template, CLAUDE.md | [Q1: PM in the AI Era](01-pm-ai-era.qmd) |
| 2 | Mon, Sep 21 | Aim | Product strategy: where to play, how to win, moats | Frontend fundamentals (HTML/CSS), deployment | [Q2: Product Strategy](product-strategy.qmd), [Sprint 1 Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#sprint-1-ship-showcase) |
| 3 | Mon, Sep 28 | Aim | What to build, and whether you should: product morality, problems worth solving | React basics, CC: slash commands | Q3: [Product Morality](product-morality.qmd) + [Problems Worth Solving](02-problems-worth-solving.qmd), **Sprint 1 Due**, [Sprint 2 Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#sprint-2-validate-commit) |
| 4 | Mon, Oct 5 | Discover | Problem discovery, Jobs-to-be-Done, the Mom Test | Client-side state, forms, CC: agents | [Q4: Problem Discovery](03-problem-discovery.qmd) |
| 5 | Mon, Oct 12 | Discover | Validating opportunities, committing to a problem | Supabase setup + auth | [Q5: Validating Opportunities](04-validating-opportunities.qmd), **Sprint 2 Due**, [Sprint 3 Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#sprint-3-product-mvp) |
| 6 | Mon, Oct 19 | Build | MVP scoping, user stories, PRDs (AI-assisted) | Supabase database, CRUD, RLS | [Q6: Building Your MVP](05-building-your-mvp.qmd) |
| 7 | Mon, Oct 26 | Build | Usability testing and platform strategy (web vs mobile) | APIs & integrations, CC: hooks and skills | Q7: [Testing with Users](06-testing-with-users.qmd) + [Platform Strategy](07-platform-strategy.qmd), [Mid-Semester Survey Due](00-assessments.qmd#mid-semester-feedback-survey) |
| 8 | Mon, Nov 2 | Build | Sustainable code: keeping what you built healthy | Refactoring, tests, code review with Claude Code | [Q8: Sustainable Code](11-sustainable-code.qmd), **Sprint 3 Due**, [Peer Code Review Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#peer-code-review) |
| 9 | Mon, Nov 9 | Grow | Product metrics, leading vs lagging indicators | Analytics setup, event tracking | [Q9: Measuring What Matters](08-measuring-what-matters.qmd), [Peer Code Review Due](00-assessments.qmd#peer-code-review), [Sprint 4 Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#sprint-4-progress-check) |
| 10 | Mon, Nov 16 | Grow | Go-to-market and business models: reaching users, making money | Landing pages, SEO basics | Q10: [Go-to-Market](09-go-to-market.qmd) + [Business Models](10-business-models.qmd) |
| 11 | Mon, Nov 23 | Scrimmage | Scrimmage: build & feedback | Build & feedback | **Sprint 4 Due**, [Sprint 5 Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#sprint-5-progress-check). Withdraw deadline is Tue, Nov 24. |
| 12 | Mon, Nov 30 | Scrimmage | Scrimmage: build & feedback | Build & feedback | **Sprint 5 Due**, [Peer Product Testing Assigned](00-assessments.qmd#peer-product-testing) |
| 13 | Mon, Dec 7 | Wrap Up | Storytelling & the final demo, course retrospective | Video prep, polish | [Peer Product Testing Due](00-assessments.qmd#peer-product-testing) |
| – | Thu, Dec 10 | – | Last day of class (no meeting) | | [Final Project Due](00-assessments.qmd#final-project) |

## Course Phases

The course runs on one loop, **AIRE**: Aim, Inform, Reason, Enact (introduced in [Chapter 1](01-pm-ai-era.qmd)). The parts below map to it: Aim is what is worth building, Discover is Inform, and Build folds Reason and Enact together as the loop turns into Grow.

| Practices | Part | Focus |
|-----------|------|-------|
| 0–1 | Foundations | Setup, the AIRE loop, how the class runs |
| 2–3 | Aim | Product strategy, product morality, problems worth solving |
| 4–5 | Discover (Inform) | Problem discovery, customer validation, commit to a problem |
| 6–8 | Build (Reason + Enact) | MVP scoping, user testing, platform strategy, sustainable code |
| 9–10 | Grow | Metrics, go-to-market, business models |
| 11–12 | Scrimmages | Self-managed sprints, peer testing, dedicated build time |
| 13 | Wrap Up | Storytelling, final demo, course retrospective |

## Scrimmages

Practices 11 and 12 are **scrimmages**: dedicated in-class sessions where you demo your product to a small group of peers, give and get feedback, and build on what you hear. A scrimmage is a working session, not a formal presentation:

1. In small groups, each person gives a 3-minute live demo of their real, deployed product.
2. The group responds: what worked, what confused them, what they would try next.
3. You spend the rest of the session building on the most useful feedback, with your group nearby to react.

Scrimmages exist because the first half of the course is mostly solo work. They are your standing chance to see what classmates are building, borrow ideas, and get fresh eyes on your product before the final. Bring something that runs.

## Sprints

| Sprint | Assigned | Due | Focus | Key Deliverables |
|--------|----------|-----|-------|------------------|
| 1 | Mon, Sep 21 | Mon, Sep 28 | Ship & Showcase | Portfolio live, 1 prototype, 5 problems |
| 2 | Mon, Sep 28 | Mon, Oct 12 | Validate & Commit | Worksheet, AI interview, customer interview, ICP |
| 3 | Mon, Oct 12 | Mon, Nov 2 | Product MVP | Spec docs, core feature, 3 user tests |
| 4 | Mon, Nov 9 | Mon, Nov 23 | Progress Check | Self-managed, 5-min Loom video |
| 5 | Mon, Nov 23 | Mon, Nov 30 | Progress Check | Self-managed, 5-min Loom video |
| Final | – | Thu, Dec 10 | Final Product | 5-min Loom video, product deployed |

## Peer Assignments

| Assignment | Due | Focus |
|------------|-----|-------|
| [Peer Code Review](00-assessments.qmd#peer-code-review) | Mon, Nov 9 | AI-assisted analysis of peer's codebase |
| [Peer Product Testing](00-assessments.qmd#peer-product-testing) | Mon, Dec 7 | Be a real test user for peer's app |

## Readings

Each chapter integrates PM/Strategy content with hands-on Build content. Read the assigned chapter(s) before the quiz date. Three weeks pair two shorter chapters under one quiz.

| Part | Chapter | Quiz Date |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Foundations | [Product Management in the AI Era](01-pm-ai-era.qmd) | Mon, Sep 14 |
| Aim | [Product Strategy](product-strategy.qmd) | Mon, Sep 21 |
| Aim | [Product Morality](product-morality.qmd) | Mon, Sep 28 |
| Aim | [Problems Worth Solving](02-problems-worth-solving.qmd) | Mon, Sep 28 |
| Discover | [Problem Discovery](03-problem-discovery.qmd) | Mon, Oct 5 |
| Discover | [Validating Opportunities](04-validating-opportunities.qmd) | Mon, Oct 12 |
| Build | [Building Your MVP](05-building-your-mvp.qmd) | Mon, Oct 19 |
| Build | [Testing with Users](06-testing-with-users.qmd) | Mon, Oct 26 |
| Build | [Platform Strategy](07-platform-strategy.qmd) | Mon, Oct 26 |
| Build | [Sustainable Code](11-sustainable-code.qmd) | Mon, Nov 2 |
| Grow | [Measuring What Matters](08-measuring-what-matters.qmd) | Mon, Nov 9 |
| Grow | [Go-to-Market](09-go-to-market.qmd) | Mon, Nov 16 |
| Grow | [Business Models](10-business-models.qmd) | Mon, Nov 16 |
| Grow | [Storytelling and the Final Demo](12-final-presentations.qmd) | – |
